content.problemStatement:
"I'm a fan of anything that tries to replace actual human contact." - Sheldon.

After years of hard work, Sheldon was finally able to develop a formula which would diminish the real human contact.

He found kkk integers n1,n2...nkn_1,n_2...n_kn1​,n2​...nk​ . Also he found that if he could minimize the value of mmm such that  ∑i=1k\sum_{i=1}^k∑i=1k​nnniiiCCCmmmiii is even, where mmm = \sum_{i=1}^k$$m_i, he would finish the real human contact.

Since Sheldon is busy choosing between PS-4 and XBOX-ONE, he want you to help him to calculate the minimum value of mmm.

###Input:


The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The
description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer kkk.
Next line contains k space separated integers n1,n2...nkn_1,n_2...n_kn1​,n2​...nk​ .
###Output:

For each test case output the minimum value of m for which ∑i=1k\sum_{i=1}^k∑i=1k​nnniiiCCCmmmiii is even, where  mmm=m1m_1m1​+m2m_2m2​+. . . mkm_kmk​   and  000 <= mim_imi​<= nin_ini​ .  If no such answer exists print -1.

###Constraints


1≤T≤10001 \leq T \leq 10001≤T≤1000
1≤k≤10001 \leq k \leq 10001≤k≤1000
1≤ni≤101 \leq n_i \leq 101≤ni​≤10181818
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The first line of each test case contains a single integer k. The next line contains k space-separated integers n1, n2, ..., nk.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the minimum value of m for which the summation of nCr is even, where m = m1 + m2 + ... + mk and 0 <= mi <= ni. If no such answer exists, print -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: 5C2 = 10 which is even and m is minimum.
Explanation: 3C2 + 4C2 = 10 which is even and m is minimum.
Explanation: 5C2 + 6C2 + 7C2 = 30 which is even and m is minimum.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a recursive approach to calculate the summation of nCr for each possible value of m. We start by initializing the minimum value of m to infinity. Then, we iterate over all possible values of m from 0 to the sum of all ni. For each value of m, we calculate the summation of nCr using a recursive function. If the summation is even, we update the minimum value of m. Finally, we return the minimum value of m. If no such answer exists, we return -1.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * k * 2^k * n) — where n is the maximum value of ni
solution.spaceComplexity: O(k) — for storing the values of ni
